guideline_references: TG (Therapeutic Guidelines) Cardiovascular — acute coronary syndromes and peripheral arterial disease
European Society for Vascular Surgery (ESVS) 2024 — Chronic Limb-Threatening Ischaemia / Acute Limb Ischaemia
intentional_test_features: Men's-health entry point (8 months of vasculogenic erectile dysfunction) as the SENTINEL of systemic atherosclerosis — the trap is anchoring on the ED complaint and missing that the acute encounter is a dual-territory ischaemic emergency (critical limb ischaemia + unstable angina).

Telehealth reprojection: source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who took vitals, auscultated a carotid bruit, palpated pulses and an LV heave, performed fundoscopy, administered chewed aspirin and a sublingual GTN spray, and called an ambulance. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, measure BP, auscultate, palpate pulses, or administer/prescribe drugs — it is scored on recognising the emergency and escalating to 000 immediately.

Over-triage resistance is NOT the lesson here — this is a genuine patient-reportable time-critical emergency (resting limb pain + new exertional chest tightness). Under-triage (managing the ED or the leg pain as routine, or reaching for a PDE5 inhibitor) is the dangerous failure and is weighted 3x.
Age not stated in the source — inferred adult (~58) from an 8-month ED history plus long-standing untreated hypertension, metabolic syndrome, xanthelasma and hypertensive retinopathy. Flagged.
Source category: GRM (Genitourinary, Renal & Men's Health), source note GRM-017.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
